dimension_notes.naming_quality: Most files follow kebab-case naming. Factory function names are descriptive but mechanical. Variable naming is consistent throughout.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Streaming handlers in use-channel.ts now use extracted helper functions (applyStreamComplete, applyStreamError, buildStreamErrorFallback). Route handler logic is well-structured with clear early returns.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Gateway-client.ts now has typed payload interfaces for all 6 broadcast wrappers instead of Record<string,unknown>. Some route handlers still use inline type assertions. TypedMessagePayload has proper optional fields.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: PROTOCOL.md is comprehensive. Internal API contracts are well-documented. Some implicit contracts between web and gateway could be more explicit.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: DELETE endpoints now consistently return {success: true}. Error responses follow {error: string} pattern. Some handlers use console.error inconsistently.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: Handler registration pattern in use-channel.ts is well-extracted. mergeServerData helper eliminates repetitive state update patterns. Route handler factories provide good DI pattern.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Codebase shows signs of iterative AI development but has been cleaned up. Some repetitive patterns remain in test files but are acceptable.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean separation of concerns: Gateway=transport, Go=orchestration, Web=API+UI. The architecture is well-documented and followed.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: ChatProvider state management improved with mergeServerData. Streaming handlers are now cleaner. Some components still have high complexity.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Helper functions extracted from long expressions. Stream handlers are now more readable. Some inline type casts remain.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clear package boundaries. Shared types in @tavok/shared. Gateway client provides clean abstraction over HTTP transport.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Environment variable reads are now at call-time (socket.ts fixed). Module-level state is minimal and well-documented.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: File naming is now consistently kebab-case. Import patterns are standardized. Some test files use slightly different mock patterns.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Dependencies are well-managed. No circular imports detected. Package boundaries are clean.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Added test suites for gateway-client.ts (16 tests), internal-api-client.ts (8 tests), agent-auth.ts (17 tests), agent-factory.ts (15 tests). Some complex modules like use-channel.ts lack unit tests due to jsdom requirement. Overall test infrastructure is solid but coverage gaps remain in hook and provider code.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: DELETE endpoints standardized to {success:true}. Route handler signatures are consistent. Internal API uses typed interfaces. Webhook dispatch follows documented protocol.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Auth patterns are consistent across route handlers: session check, permission check, then action. Internal auth uses x-internal-secret header uniformly.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: PascalCase to kebab-case rename is complete. All .js files have been migrated to .ts. No stale migration artifacts remain.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Auth files follow consistent naming in lib/. Components are well-organized in feature directories. Test files colocated with source.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Consistent patterns: route handlers, streaming lifecycle, broadcast helpers. mergeServerData pattern could be applied more broadly.

issues.title: use-channel.ts (760 LOC) has no unit tests
issues.description: The main real-time hook has zero unit test coverage. The extracted helper functions (applyStreamComplete, applyStreamError, buildStreamErrorFallback) are pure and testable but not yet tested.
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for the pure helper functions. Consider adding jsdom environment support to test hook behavior.

issues.title: ChatProvider component has no test coverage
issues.description: The main state management provider for the chat UI has no unit tests. The mergeServerData helper is pure and testable.
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for mergeServerData helper at minimum.

issues.title: Several route handlers use inline 'as' type assertions for request body parsing
issues.description: Route handlers like channel PATCH use 'as Record<string, unknown>' after JSON parsing rather than runtime validation.
issues.suggestion: Consider using zod or similar for runtime body validation.

issues.title: Some handlers use console.error with different message formats
issues.description: Error logging uses inconsistent formats: some prefix with function name, some use template literals, some use string concatenation.
issues.suggestion: Standardize error logging format across route handlers.

issues.title: stream_thinking handler still uses inline map expression
issues.description: While stream_complete and stream_error were extracted to helpers, the stream_thinking handler still uses inline map logic.
issues.suggestion: Extract applyStreamThinking helper for consistency with other stream helpers.
